purpose: Define language as the required interaction-output channel, not as translated benchmark rules.

EN.assigned_only: Output-channel requirement: your assigned channel is EN. Write every visible negotiation message in English only. Keep protocol tags such as OFFER:, ACCEPT:, REJECT:, and XML field names exactly as specified.
EN.free_choice: Output-channel requirement: EN is allowed for this pair. You may write visible negotiation messages in English or the other allowed language for this pair. Keep protocol tags such as OFFER:, ACCEPT:, REJECT:, and XML field names exactly as specified.

ID.assigned_only: Output-channel requirement: your assigned channel is ID. Write every visible negotiation message in Indonesian only. Keep protocol tags such as OFFER:, ACCEPT:, REJECT:, and XML field names exactly as specified.
ID.free_choice: Output-channel requirement: ID is allowed for this pair. You may write visible negotiation messages in Indonesian or the other allowed language for this pair. Keep protocol tags such as OFFER:, ACCEPT:, REJECT:, and XML field names exactly as specified.

ZH.assigned_only: Output-channel requirement: your assigned channel is ZH. Write every visible negotiation message in Simplified Chinese only. Keep protocol tags such as OFFER:, ACCEPT:, REJECT:, and XML field names exactly as specified.
ZH.free_choice: Output-channel requirement: ZH is allowed for this pair. You may write visible negotiation messages in Simplified Chinese or the other allowed language for this pair. Keep protocol tags such as OFFER:, ACCEPT:, REJECT:, and XML field names exactly as specified.

conditions.C0: Both agents output only Lx during interaction.
conditions.C1: Both agents output only Ly during interaction.
conditions.C2: Agents are assigned different output languages and must output only their assigned language.
conditions.C3: Agents may choose either language in the pair; measure which channel dominates.

notes: Do not block EN-ID or ZH runs on translated benchmark-rule packs.
The required validation is channel compliance in the interaction transcript: assigned-language share, code-switch rate, and off-pair language share.
Rules may remain in English unless a later experiment explicitly studies rule-language effects.
